case_name: The Case of the Abducted Zebrafish (FOR500)

_provenance.evidence_source: Organizer-provided SANS FOR500 case image (physical Surface 3 / CyLR triage).
_provenance.ground_truth_basis: DERIVED from the case scenario's explicitly stated facts — the \StarkResearch\Level 5-8 Classified paths, the 2016-06-22..30 window, the zebrafish / cell-regeneration / DNA-splice research topics, and the three investigative questions. This is NOT an official artifact answer key (USB serials / exact filenames / confirmed channel were not published).
_provenance.how_to_trust_this: Do not trust the SCORE on faith — verify the EVIDENCE. Every DeepSIFT claim is grounded per-token against the cited raw tool output and recorded in a hash-chained (optionally HMAC-signed) audit log. Run `python3 verify_findings.py` to independently re-confirm that each claim traces to intact, audited evidence. Scoring here is identical for Protocol SIFT and DeepSIFT.
_provenance.scoring_is_content_based: must_identify uses content indicator groups over the findings' text values; prose alone may satisfy a criterion only where the brief states the fact.

subject: Anthony Vanko (vanko), Biochemical Engineer, Stark Enterprises DC R&D

evidence.physical_disk_image: surface_physical.E01 (EnCase, 21 segments — full physical Surface 3 disk)

investigative_questions: Was Vanko involved with dissemination of classified information?
Validate whether Vanko copied a large volume from the StarkResearch server.
What was done with the data (exfiltration channel)?

must_identify.name: Access to classified StarkResearch server directories (Level 5-8 Classified)
Classified research subject matter accessed (zebrafish / cell regeneration / DNA splice)
Exfiltration channel evidence (removable media / cloud / comms upload)
Activity within the incident window (June 2016) tied to a file/transfer/URL artifact

bonus_identify: Specific exfiltrated file names / classified document titles
USB device make / model / serial number used for exfiltration
Upload to the Chinese university file share (.cn domain / external host)
Communications (Skype / WhatsApp / email) referencing the data or recipient
Timeline reconstruction across multiple disk artifacts
should_not_hallucinate: Specific stolen file names without supporting artifact evidence
USB serial numbers or device IDs not present in registry/setupapi
Network connections or IPs not observed in disk artifacts
Exfiltration dates not grounded in artifact timestamps
Attacker/recipient identity beyond what the evidence shows
scoring_criteria._scoring_note: Scenario-DERIVED ground truth (built from the FOR500 brief's explicit facts: the \StarkResearch\Level 5-8 Classified\ paths, the June 22-30 2016 window, the zebrafish/cell-regeneration topics, and the three investigative questions). It is NOT an official answer key — no definitive artifact list (USB serials, exact filenames, confirmed exfil channel) was available on the box at authoring time. must_identify uses CONTENT-based indicator groups: a criterion is satisfied only when each group has >=1 hit in the findings TEXT VALUES (prose alone may satisfy where the brief states the fact). Indicators prefixed 're:' are case-insensitive REGEX. Tighten these once the first run reveals the actual extracted artifacts. Identical scoring for both Protocol SIFT and DeepSIFT.
scoring_criteria._window_note: Incident window: classified docs posted to Chinese server 2016-06-22/23; bulk copy from StarkResearch detected 2016-06-30.
